title: Upstart Q1 2026 slides: 44% revenue growth amid margin pressure
text: Upstart is projecting total revenue of approximately $1.4 billion, revenue from fees of $1.3 billion, and adjusted EBITDA of $294 million representing a 21% margin for full-year 2026. Contribution margin came in at 50% in Q1 2026, down three percentage points from the prior quarter, reflecting the mix, seasonality, and marketing investment dynamics. The company expects contribution margin in Q1 will be the low point for the year, barring any changes to the macroeconomic environment. For the full year, the company continues to expect growth in absolute contribution profit dollars to be within at least five percentage points of fee revenue growth. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be weighted toward the second half of 2026, driven by origination growth and operating expense leverage. The company indicates that fixed cost investments for the year were front-loaded into Q1, and they expect more modest sequential growth for the remainder of 2026. This positions them for adjusted EBITDA margin acceleration as the year progresses. Q1 Profitability Decline: Despite strong origination growth, Q1 profitability declined marginally due to front-loaded expenses and product mix shifts.
